title: allium vineale subsp. compactum
intro: Allium vineale subsp. compactum, also known as the wild garlic or meadow garlic, is a plant species that belongs to the Allium genus and is characterized by its compact growth habit and strong onion-like aroma. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antibacterial and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its sulfur compounds. The medicinal properties of Allium vineale subsp. compactum have been found to have health benefits, including lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and acting as an anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and digestive issues.
